mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly reducing present values and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns exceed 55% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the unemployment rate crosses the threshold without a corresponding drop in SPX within 5 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and economic momentum, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts yield a negative or flat 60-day forward return in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, causing equity markets to price in lower future earnings and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production changes during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 65% of observations, invalidating the slowdown pricing mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate profit expectations, which consistently supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 2.5% year-over-year, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that will suppress economic growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Elevated CPI readings fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with positive outcomes occurring in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand despite aggregate growth, prompting equity market repricing and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence yields positive 20-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply-side bottlenecks that delay economic normalization, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Large industrial production drops during recovery phases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or the regime-specific signal shows no statistical edge over a baseline model.
